title: Paradox Interactive Launches Major Expansion and Free Update for Stellaris
used_for: 4.4 Pegasus and Nomads launch date; Arkships, Waylines, Contracts, join/leave wars, job-system and Situation Log/UI changes
notes: Lines observed: Jun 15 2026 launch, Nomads + free 4.4 Pegasus update; features include Nomadic Empires, Arkships, Wayline Networks, Contract System.

title: Steam Community: Stellaris 4.4.4 patch released (5505)
used_for: latest stable patch status; 4.4.4 download availability and checksum; 4.4.4 bugfix implications for Arkship/Waystation/Nomad compatibility
notes: Steam all-news page showed title “Stellaris 4.4.4 patch released (5505)”, Jun 24, and says 4.4.4 ready for Steam, GOG, MS Store.

title: Steam Community: Stellaris 4.4.5 “Pegasus” Early Open Beta (c22e) now available
used_for: 4.4.5 beta branch status; early beta caveats; Resource Abundance, Waystation Voidlure, Arkship Voidlure, automation changes
notes: Steam all-news page showed Jun 25 early beta on stellaris_test with minimal QA warning and ongoing notes.

title: SteamDB mirror of Stellaris Dev Diary #427 - Second Open Beta
used_for: updated 4.4.5 beta modding changes; 4.5 Cygnus open beta warnings and modding changes
notes: SteamDB mirrors Steam Community notes. It showed 2 July 2026 update text: 4.4.5 beta on stellaris_test, situation/custom_tooltip/starbase/automation/home_colony additions, and 4.5 pop ethics/faction breaking change.

title: Paradox Interactive: Stellaris Update 4.0
used_for: 4.0 Phoenix structural break: pop groups, workforce, species modification, trade/logistics, planet UI, district specializations
notes: Paradox explains pop grouping, workforce assigned to jobs, simultaneous growth, trade route removal, trade/logistics resource, and planet UI/jobs/district specializations.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Patch 4.0
used_for: Patch 4.0 feature inventory and modding impact matrix
notes: Wiki says 4.0 Phoenix was released 2025-05-05 with checksum 1ae3, notes 4.0 was not public and 4.0.1 was first public 4.0.x branch; lists planetary economy rework, Pop Groups & Workforce, trade revamp, etc.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Modding tutorial
used_for: descriptor files, Windows storage path, folder mirroring, common folders, naming, CWTools/tool recommendations, logs, launch parameters, debug workflow, event basics, Git recommendation
notes: Important lines: two descriptors; outer .mod path in Documents/.../mod, inner descriptor.mod no path; supported_version is visual only; never edit install folder; mirror install structure; tools/logs/debug flags.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Localisation modding
used_for: localisation folder spelling, UTF-8 BOM, filename suffix, first-line language key, no fallback language, localisation/replace LIOS overwrite behavior
notes: Page warns some sections older, but basics are stable and current in practice.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Scopes
used_for: scope model, THIS/PREV/FROM/ROOT, exists checks, trigger/effect scope errors, any_/every_/random_ behavior, event targets
notes: Page notes some sections older, so use trigger_docs/current vanilla script docs for final validation.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Console commands
used_for: console access, non-Ironman restriction, debugtooltip ID inspection, achievements disabled, help command
notes: Page verified for PC 4.2; report flags command names as requiring in-console verification on 4.4.x.

title: Irony Mod Manager homepage
used_for: Irony features, supported Stellaris, FIOS/LIOS understanding, deterministic load-order management, conflict awareness
notes: Homepage says Irony originally built for Stellaris and understands game structures, FIOS/LIOS, deterministic load order.

title: Steam Community discussion: Irony Mod Manager advanced conflict solver
used_for: FIOS/LIOS explanation; why filename-only conflict checking is inadequate; patch mod generation; not every conflict needs patching
notes: Useful for practical load-order behavior, but not official Paradox docs.

title: CWTools: Get started
used_for: VS Code extension setup, open mod folder, select vanilla folder, scan errors, use Git, Windows/Linux mod-folder examples
notes: CWTools page explicitly recommends backups/Git and shows Windows/Linux mod folders.

title: cwtools-action GitHub repository
used_for: automated PDXScript validation in CI; dependency on validation rules and vanilla cache; full cache needed for accurate load-order/file-override validation
notes: Good for professional mod maintenance workflows.

title: GitHub: 3v4d3/GalacticConclave
used_for: LLM diplomacy companion architecture: save parser, LLM client, command file + run command bridge, local/cloud LLM support, Ironman restriction
notes: Small/new repo; claims need verification by running code, but README is direct source for project architecture.

title: GitHub: Dfintz/Stellaris_Overmind
used_for: LLM strategic advisor and AI-empires macro-strategy steering; save polling; personality/stat overrides while native AI executes micro decisions
notes: README reports version-locked knowledge examples and specific architecture. Treat as prototype until tested.

title: GitHub: gitmaan/stellaris-companion
used_for: AI save analyzer / strategic advisor, MCP relay into Claude/Codex, local save privacy, Rust parser
notes: External advisor rather than runtime empire controller.

title: Steam Workshop: Beachboys Fair AI 2
used_for: example of AI-assisted mod-development claims and community skepticism; not evidence of runtime LLM in-game
notes: Listing claims designed with ChatGPT; comments allege syntax/structure issues. Useful as warning: AI assistance requires vanilla-schema validation.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Mods search result / mod-folder OS paths
type: official community wiki search-result snippet; page challenge blocked full open
used_for: Linux and macOS mod-folder paths when full page was blocked by client challenge
notes: Search snippet showed Linux ~/.local/share/Paradox Interactive/Stellaris/mod/ and Mac OS ~/Documents/Paradox Interactive/Stellaris/mod/.

title: Stellaris Wiki: Save-game editing search result / save paths
used_for: save-game path background for external tools; not central to mod folder rules
notes: Used only for save-path context and external AI tooling.
